---
title: Self Landing Rocket Using Reinforcement Learning
---

## Introduction

Rocket landing is a challenging nonlinear control problem. A vehicle descending under gravity must regulate position, velocity, and orientation simultaneously, while operating under actuator and fuel constraints. Small control errors compound quickly, making a stable touchdown difficult without precise feedback and coordination.

This project explores whether reinforcement learning can learn that control strategy directly from interaction with a simulated environment. Using a custom 2D physics engine built with Gymnasium and rendered in PyGame, agents are trained to land a rocket upright and within a defined pad region. Training is performed with Ray RLlib using both DQN and PPO. The learned policies discover when to rotate, when to fire the main engine, and how to reduce velocity for a controlled landing without any hardcoded landing logic.

---

## Motivation

Reinforcement learning treats landing as a sequential decision problem. The agent observes the rocket’s full state, takes discrete thrust actions, and receives shaped rewards encouraging stability, fuel-aware control, and precise touchdown. Over many episodes, it learns a control policy purely from reward feedback.

The project is built around four core design elements:

- A 7-dimensional continuous state space capturing position, velocity, angle, angular velocity, and remaining fuel  
- A shaped reward function with dense mid-air penalties and tiered landing bonuses  
- Distributed RLlib training pipelines for both DQN and PPO  
- A discrete thruster-based action space reflecting realistic actuation choices  

By combining structured reward shaping with modern deep RL algorithms, the agent learns coordinated behaviors such as stabilizing orientation early, correcting horizontal drift during descent, and reducing velocity near the ground before committing to landing.

---

## GitHub Repository

The GitHub repository hosting all code for this project can be found [here](https://github.com/dcorc7/Self-Landing-Rocket).
